name: Grilled Lobster Dinner
recipeInstructions:
1. Light the grill.
2. Fill a large bowl halfway with cool water. Add the ears of corn with husks intact and let soak for 20 minutes.
3. In a small saucepan, combine butter and garlic and melt over low heat. Stir in oregano, cayenne, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Cover and set aside.
4. Using the blunt edge of a knife, crack the lobster claws and knuckles on one side without crushing the meat. Brush the shell of the lobster body and the uncracked sides of the claws and knuckles with olive oil.
5. Place the lobster pieces cracked side up on a platter and brush with some reserved herb butter.
6. Drain the ears of corn and place them in the center of the grill. Cover and grill for 5 minutes.
7. Add the lobster pieces, cracked side up, to the grill and cover. Grill occasionally brushing with herb butter for 15 minutes.
8. Brush the ears of corn thoroughly with fresh water and rotate slightly to blacken evenly.
9. Add the clams to the hottest spot on the grill. Cover and grill about 5 minutes until clams open.
10. Leave unopened clams on the grill while transferring the opened clams, corn, and lobster to a large platter.
11. Using tongs, check the lobster for doneness by pulling up the tail meat and checking the underside; it should be firm and dark orange. Return to grill if required (do not overcook!).
12. Remove remaining clams from the grill, discard any unopened.
13. Brush the clams and lobster tails with more herb butter.
14. Peel and discard husks and silks from corn; brush with herb butter.
15. Garnish with lemon wedges and serve; pass remaining butter for dipping.
